scope: Scope E — high-risk rows must not become editable without a real recovery strategy.

summary.recoveryMechanismShipped: in-window dead-man rollback modal (adw::Dialog): capture original -> apply live -> 15s countdown -> Keep/Revert, with Revert on timeout/Escape/close/default; a Kept change joins the unified pending ledger and Discard reverts it through the same controller

deadManRecoveryEnabled.how: rows classified DeadManPreviewCandidate that passed a per-row live proof (apply+revert verified against the running compositor with a usable fallback input path). The arm control applies the value live, then the modal owns the countdown and resolution.

guiProven.keptJoinedPendingLedger: chip/bar/discard all appear; runtime stays int:0

backendTests: tests/dead_man_rollback_dialog.rs: timeout reverts, revert_now reverts, keep prevents timeout revert, close/Escape (revert_if_unconfirmed) reverts, keep then close does not revert, countdown starts at 15 and is bound to the executor timeout, dialog title/message wording matches the reference

monitorDisplay.reason: a display-specific rollback flow (capture monitor name/resolution/refresh/position/scale/transform/enabled + original monitor rule, apply temporary, verify changed, revert-verify) is not proven on this machine; a bad monitor apply can black or mislayout the only display, and this compositor's monitor set is lua-driven with no verified revert path. The Monitors page renders the monitor line and every render/opengl option read-only / Blocked ('Extra care needed').
monitorDisplay.displayDialogVariantBuiltAndTested: dead_man_dialog_title(true)='Keep these display settings?' and dead_man_dialog_message(true, n)='Reverting to previous display settings in n seconds.' are implemented and unit-tested, ready for the day a proven monitor-rollback flow exists; no monitor row is armable today, so the variant has no live trigger.

screenShader.reason: decoration.screen_shader stays high-risk/blocked: a bad shader can black the display with no in-band recovery. Existing screen_shader_advisory scaffolding is non-live/advisory only.

inputSessionLockFocus.enabled: input.* pointer/scroll/focus rows that PASSED a per-row live proof are armable through the dead-man modal (38 rows). These are behavioral input changes with a verified fallback path.
inputSessionLockFocus.blocked: input rows without a passed live proof, and session-lock / focus rows that could strand input, remain blocked or hardware-gated. No blind-recovery claim is made without proof.

safety.runtimeMutated: only via the app's own reversible dead-man preview during the live proof; every change reverted and cross-checked with getoption (repeat_rate returned to int:25)
